title: NOC 42100 – Police Officers (Except Commissioned) | GTR Immigration
description: Police Officers (Except Commissioned) under NOC 42100 are front-line public safety professionals responsible for enforcing laws, maintaining public order, preventing crime, and protecting communities across Canada. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation requires specialized training, physical fitness, and completion of police academy programs. Police officers work in municipal police services, provincial police forces, federal agencies such as the RCMP, transportation police units, and other law enforcement agencies.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group42 – Front-line public protection services and paraprofessional occupationsSub-Major Group421 – Occupations in front-line public protection servicesMinor Group4210 – Occupations in front-line public protection servicesNOC Code42100

duties: Respond to emergency calls and complaints
Execute search warrants and arrest suspects
Patrol assigned areas on foot, bicycle, horseback, or motor vehicles
Prepare incident reports and testify in court
Provide emergency assistance to accident and crime victims
Enforce traffic laws and conduct DUI testing
Participate in crime prevention and community outreach programs
Maintain equipment and law enforcement records
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in police technology:Required in QuebecUsually required in other provinces
Completion of a 3–6 month police training program
Must meet:Physical fitness standardsAgility and strength requirementsVision standardsPsychological assessment requirements
Specialized certifications may be required for certain policing units.
Note: Canadian citizenship or permanent residency is typically required for police service recruitment.
salary_prospects: Police Officers (Except Commissioned) typically earn between:
$55,600 – $111,600 per year,depending on experience, rank, and province.
Senior constables, sergeants, and specialized unit officers may earn higher salaries.
job_outlook: Government law enforcement remains a critical and stable sector across Canada.
labour_market_demand: Police Officers under NOC 42100 are employed by:
Increased focus on public safety and cybercrime
Strong job security in government sector
Opportunities for specialization (forensics, highway patrol, K-9, cybercrime)
Police services across Canada frequently recruit qualified candidates, particularly in growing urban and regional communities.

However, specific eligibility depends on citizenship requirements and provincial recruitment policies.
